attacking_style: Late-climb attacker who typically moves inside final 5km, especially on steep gradients in final 500m-2.5km. Prefers tactical positioning over long-range moves, showing patience to counter or sprint from reduced groups.
team_tactics: Team plants domestiques ahead for crucial pacing on decisive climbs (Kemner 2022 Giro). Benefits from dual GC card strategy creating tactical complexity. Tempo control less emphasized than strategic positioning.
terrain_preference: Excels on steep-gradient finishes where gradients kick up in final kilometers. Strong on multi-climb mountain stages where positioning and late accelerations matter more than sustained power. Sprint from reduced groups is a weapon.
vulnerability: Time trialing is critical weakness - lost 39 seconds in 15.7km ITT when GC-tied (2020), though improved by 2022 to match Carapaz. Cramping issues suggest pacing or recovery problems when racing into form.
key_patterns: Launches attacks inside final 5km on decisive climbs, not long-range moves (2022 Giro, UAE Tour)
Superior on steep gradients, particularly final 500m kicks (Dauphine 2023, UAE Tour 2020)
Strong positioning sense in finale: won corner battle for sprint, took bonus seconds via photo finish
Time trial weakness improved 2020-2022: lost 39s to Geoghegan Hart, matched Carapaz within 1s two years later
Tactically patient: wheel-sits stronger riders before late attacks (Stelvio 2020, followed Nibali chase 2022)
Cramping under pressure suggests form/pacing vulnerabilities when building condition (Romandie 2023)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Tour podium confirmed Giro form translates beyond single race, but Romandie cramping suggests inconsistent condition. Peak performance remains Giro-focused but broader GC threat emerging.
